message:
chore(beads): reconcile JSONL after force-flush drift

`br sync --flush-only --force` exported only 1044 of 1047 issues,
silently dropping bd-8sgm6, bd-9chy.10, and bd-o4yda from the JSONL
even though the SQLite DB contained all three with identical metadata
to the healthy cohort (no ephemeral/template/deleted_at flags). This
is a `br` export bug: direct `sqlite3`-equivalent queries via python
confirmed all 1047 rows were present with the expected states.

Recovered the three lost records by:
1. Pulling their full JSONL lines from git HEAD (pre-flush).
2. Reconciling each record's status/updated_at/closed_at fields
   against the DB's authoritative state so the JSONL reflects the
   real closures:
   - bd-8sgm6  (closed, cc-libc — duplicate of bd-1tvbi, see notes)
   - bd-9chy.10 (closed by another agent)
   - bd-o4yda  (in_progress by another agent)
3. Carrying over the bd-8sgm6 `notes` field (1157 chars) so the
   "duplicate of bd-1tvbi" close rationale lives in JSONL too.

JSONL line count back to 1047. All six recent target beads verified
at expected status (qij6l/c5aak/ni101/8sgm6/9chy.10 closed, o4yda
in_progress).

Agent: cc-libc
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:22:01.082998283Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T03:57:34.998390505Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T03:57:34.996153142Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Add focused ABI regression coverage so the forbidden all-ones payload contract is frozen exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:39:08.811494381Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:41:19.937299060Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T08:41:19.934949842Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity eventfd_write(valid_fd, UINT64_MAX) behavior: exact EINVAL and new ABI regression coverage; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-8sa88","title":"[MODE-1][HIGH] symbolic correctness: c11threads create/join backend mismatch","description":"[MODE-1][HIGH] symbolic correctness: C11 thread create/join backend contract is internally inconsistent

Validated by current remote gate failure:
- `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cod rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- failing suite: `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/c11threads_abi_test.rs`

Concrete mismatch:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:72-110` calls `crate::pthread_abi::pthread_create`, which prefers host `pthread_create` whenever `resolved_thread_create_raw()` is available (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1991-2015`).
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:118-124` then calls `crate::pthread_abi::pthread_join`.
- but `pthread_join` is hard-routed to `native_pthread_join` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:2019-2021`), which only accepts handles present in `THREAD_HANDLE_REGISTRY` and returns `ESRCH` otherwise (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1409-1445`).

For host-created threads, the symbolic contract `join(create(f, arg)) = THRD_SUCCESS + propagated return value` is broken: the handle identity model used by `create` is not the one consumed by `join`.

Observed fallout already matches that model break:
- `test_thrd_create_join`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_zero`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_negative`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_large`

Fix direction:
- make C11 thread lifecycle choose one backend consistently across create/join/detach/self/equal, or
- teach `pthread_join` to resolve host-thread handles through the host path instead of assuming every successful create registered a native handle.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:43:44.824287281Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:28:24.610214936Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:28:24.608033863Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of bd-21ypi / bd-jqy64 — same c11threads create/join backend mismatch. Fix landed in commit 138c07c3 (pthread_join now delegates to host for non-managed handles). c11threads_abi_test suite is green (36/0). bd-jqy64 remains in_progress under cod's ownership.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["c11threads","modes","review","symbolic"]}
-{"id":"bd-8sgm6","title":"[MODE-3][MEDIUM] ABI compatibility: printf executor hardcodes LP64 widths","description":"[MODE-3][MEDIUM] ABI compatibility: printf conformance executor hardcodes LP64 vararg widths

Recent printf executor work widened promotion and dispatch handling, but the ABI model is still host-width-specific:
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:2492-2496` stores long-like integers as `PrintfArg::Long(c_longlong)` and `PrintfArg::Ulong(c_ulonglong)`.
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:2502-2585` treats `%l`, `%j`, `%z`, `%t`, and `%q` as a single `long_like` bucket and rewrites integer args into those 64-bit slots.

That matches the current LP64 host, but it is not an ABI-stable model for width-sensitive formats such as `%ld`, `%zd`, and `%td`. On non-LP64 targets the executor can mis-marshal arguments and report false conformance parity.

This is an ABI review finding rather than a current Linux x86_64 failure, but it directly affects whether the conformance harness can be trusted when the matrix expands past today's host ABI.

Fix direction:
- split `long`, `long long`, `size_t`, `ptrdiff_t`, and `intmax_t` by actual target width
- source widths from explicit target metadata instead of one 64-bit promotion bucket.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:46:05.341784917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:46:05.341784917Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","modes","printf","review"]}
 {"id":"bd-8sho","title":"bd-1j4 subtask: Hard-parts docs/parity/support-matrix truth reconciliation","description":"Background:

Goal:
- Reconcile hard-parts implementation reality with user-facing claims and machine-generated reports.

Deliverables:
1) Hard-parts status truth table across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality report.
2) Drift guard updates for hard-parts claim consistency.
3) Explicit “deferred vs implemented” rationale for each hard-parts subsystem.

Acceptance Criteria:
- No contradictory hard-parts claims across docs/artifacts.
- Drift gates catch future inconsistency automatically.

Verification & Logging:
- Drift guard tests and fixtures.
- Structured logs for reconciliation checks and mismatches.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedMaple","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:33.576518493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:52:20.345066285Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:52:20.345031921Z","close_reason":"Completed hard-parts truth table reconciliation across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality_report with new drift guard script, harness test, and CI wiring.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","docs","drift","hard-parts","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1063,"issue_id":"bd-8sho","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RedMaple claiming bd-8sho and starting hard-parts truth reconciliation across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality_report.\n\nPlanned deliverables:\n1)  artifact with per-subsystem implemented vs deferred rationale.\n2)  drift guard script.\n3)  guard test.\n4) doc updates in  and  to include explicit hard-parts truth lines tied to the artifact.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:48:02Z"},{"id":1064,"issue_id":"bd-8sho","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (RedMaple): completed hard-parts docs/parity/support/reality truth reconciliation with drift guard wiring.\n\nImplemented:\n- New canonical artifact: tests/conformance/hard_parts_truth_table.v1.json\n  - per-subsystem status + implemented/deferred scope rationale for startup/threading/resolver/nss/locale/iconv\n  - support_matrix expectations per subsystem\n  - reality_report snapshot binding + contradiction summary\n- New drift guard: scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n  - validates artifact schema, README + FEATURE_PARITY hard-parts truth lines, support_matrix expectations, and reality_report snapshot alignment\n  - emits structured JSON event with trace_id/mode/api_family/outcome/artifact refs\n- New harness test: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/hard_parts_truth_test.rs\n  - artifact schema validation\n  - guard script execution gate\n- Docs reconciled:\n  - README.md hard-parts truth section added\n  - FEATURE_PARITY.md hard-parts truth section added\n- CI wiring:\n  - scripts/ci.sh now runs scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n\nVerification executed (PASS):\n- scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-codex-harness cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test hard_parts_truth_test\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-codex-harness cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test parity_report_drift_test\n\nNote:\n- /tmp is full on this host; verification used CARGO_TARGET_DIR under /data/tmp for deterministic local runs.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:52:17Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-8zsp","title":"Smoke-fuzz proptests for inet IP parsers","description":"parse_ipv4, parse_ipv6, inet_pton, inet_aton in frankenlibc_core::inet are classic security-sensitive parsers. Add Archetype-1 smoke-fuzz proptests: (a) never-panics on arbitrary bytes, (b) output bounded, (c) format-roundtrip for inputs that parse successfully (parse(format(x)) == x). Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/inet/mod.rs #[cfg(test)] module.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:29:16.512627925Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:31:46.178725431Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T20:31:46.176686404Z","close_reason":"Added 8 inet smoke-fuzz proptests in commit 0cf34de: parse_ipv4 (never-panics, structured-alphabet, round-trip), parse_ipv6 (never-panics, structured-alphabet, round-trip), inet_pton (af-cube), inet_aton (legacy). ~4100 case evaluations per run, no panics found on first sweep.  {"id":"bd-95tq","title":"resolv: gai_cancel and gai_suspend empty requests should return EAI_ALLDONE","description":"Concrete regression: host glibc returns EAI_ALLDONE with errno unchanged for synchronous-degenerate async DNS calls like gai_cancel(NULL), gai_cancel(&zeroed_gaicb), gai_suspend(NULL, 0, timeout), and gai_suspend([NULL], 1, timeout). FrankenLibC currently returns EAI_SYSTEM with ENOSYS. Restore host-parity degenerate semantics in unistd_abi.rs and lock them with focused ABI tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:14:54.765525235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:19:52.577129952Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:19:52.574907623Z","close_reason":"Restored host-parity EAI_ALLDONE semantics for synchronous-degenerate gai_cancel/gai_suspend inputs and verified locally plus via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"bd-9chy.10","title":"stdio: export _IO_2_1_stdin_/stdout_/stderr_ aliases bound to NativeFile storage","description":"## Background

Plan v4 §3 (final paragraph). Some statically-linked-against-libc binaries resolve _IO_2_1_stdin_, _IO_2_1_stdout_, _IO_2_1_stderr_ directly via dlsym or link-time resolution, bypassing the public stdin/stdout/stderr aliases. Without exporting these names from FrankenLibC, the binary sees two distinct streams (one native FrankenLibC, one host glibc) and the program misbehaves subtly: partial flushes, lost output, double-close.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1 (NativeFile layout)

## What to do

1. Add the three symbol names to crates/frankenlibc-abi/version_scripts/libc.map under the GLIBC_2.2.5 version tag.
2. In stdio_abi.rs (or io_internal_abi.rs), declare the underscored names as #[no_mangle] static aliases pointing to the *same* NativeFile storage as the public stdin/stdout/stderr globals. Do NOT allocate a second set of NativeFile objects — they must be the same object so a flush via stdin reaches the same buffer as a flush via _IO_2_1_stdin_.
3. Verify the alias by building the .so and inspecting nm: nm -D target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so | grep _IO_2_1 must show the three symbols at the same address as stdin/stdout/stderr.
4. Add an integration test: build a small C program that takes both stdin and _IO_2_1_stdin_, asserts they are equal, and runs it under LD_PRELOAD.

## Done

- libc.map exports the three aliases
- nm verification passes
- Integration test passes
- The aliases survive a release build with strip+lto","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:28:41.061649453Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:44:25.584876183Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:44:25.582645349Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","stdio","version-script"]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.15","title":"stdio: build NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T vtable + 17 trampolines for indirect dispatch","description":"## Background

Plan v4 §10. Many glibc-built binaries (especially with -fno-plt) call vfprintf indirectly through ((struct _IO_FILE_plus *)fp)->vtable->__xsputn(...) rather than through the PLT-resolved vfprintf symbol. If our vtable pointer is null or wrong, the program crashes inside __xsputn.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1 (NativeFile must already have a vtable slot)

## What to do

1. In crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/io_internal_abi.rs, define repr(C) struct _IO_jump_t with the 17 standard slots: __dummy, __dummy2, __finish, __overflow, __underflow, __uflow, __pbackfail, __xsputn, __xsgetn, __seekoff, __seekpos, __setbuf, __sync, __doallocate, __read, __write, __seek, __close, __stat, __showmanyc, __imbue. Field offsets pinned to glibc 2.34.
2. Create a static NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T: _IO_jump_t = ... initialized with extern "C" function pointers to Rust trampolines.
3. Each trampoline takes a *mut FILE, casts to *mut NativeFile, acquires the recursive lock, and dispatches to the native kernel function used by the public ABI entrypoint. The trampolines for __xsputn and __xsgetn are the high-volume ones; benchmark them.
4. Every NativeFile constructor (bd-9chy.5..10) sets `self.vtable = &NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T as *const _ as *mut _`.
5. Trampoline must handle the foreign-pointer-adoption case: if the FILE* it receives has the bloom precheck saying "not ours," route through adopt_foreign_file (separate bead bd-9chy.16). For now, panic with a clear message; the next bead wires real adoption.
6. Unit test: build a small Rust test that takes &mut NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T.__xsputn, calls it with a NativeFile* and verifies bytes are written.

## Done

- _IO_jump_t struct defined with offsets matching glibc
- NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T static built and wired into NativeFile constructors
- All 17 trampolines have implementations (some can be no-ops or default behavior)
- Unit test for indirect __xsputn dispatch passes
- Snapshot test tests/runtime_math/golden/io_jump_dispatch.v1.json captures vtable-vs-PLT call counts on a representative workload","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:30:03.906540102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T17:38:04.026649614Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T17:38:04.026388970Z","close_reason":"Implemented _IO_jump_t vtable with 21 trampolines for indirect dispatch. All slots wired to native stdio functions. NativeFile::new() sets vtable to NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T. Layout tests added. Also fixed TLS corruption and type mismatches.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["stdio","vtable"]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.16","title":"stdio: implement adopt_foreign_file via membrane bloom precheck + fd extraction","description":"## Background

Plan v4 §11. When a dlopend library opens a file BEFORE LD_PRELOAD takes effect and then hands the FILE* back to the LD_PRELOAD-ed binary, the pointer is host glibcs, not ours. Today the code "handles" this by delegating to host stdio (which is exactly the problem the epic exists to fix). Option B requires we *adopt* such pointers into the native registry instead.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.15 (vtable trampolines need a place to call the adoption path)

## What to do

1. In crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs (or io_internal_abi.rs), add `pub(crate) fn adopt_foreign_file(fp: *mut FILE) -> *mut NativeFile`.
2. Step 1 of every public stdio entrypoint AND every vtable trampoline: run the membrane bloom-filter ownership precheck (existing primitive in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/bloom.rs). Cost: ~10 ns. Well within strict-mode budget.
3. If precheck says "ours," dispatch to the kernel directly.
4. If precheck says "not ours," call adopt_foreign_file:
   a. Read ((_IO_FILE *)fp)->_fileno to recover the underlying fd. (We do NOT read other fields — they may be in a different layout from glibcs.) The _fileno offset is fixed at offset 0x70 in glibc 2.34 for x86_64; pin this constant in code with a comment explaining why we deliberately read it raw.
   b. Allocate a NativeFile shell, set its fd to the recovered value, set buffering mode based on isatty(fileno) (raw syscall), set orientation = Undecided, vtable = &NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T.
   c. Insert into stream registry indexed by BOTH the original foreign pointer AND the new native pointer (so subsequent calls with the foreign pointer find the same NativeFile).
   d. Emit a FOREIGN_FILE_ADOPTED evidence record (uses the schema from bd-9chy.4).
   e. Return the new NativeFile *.
5. Generation safety: if the same fd is later closed and reopened, the new NativeFile gets a fresh generation; the old foreign-pointer registry entry is invalidated. Subsequent stdio calls against the stale foreign pointer return EBADF and emit FOREIGN_FILE_STALE.
6. Test: build a test C program that calls dlopen("libc.so.6") and uses dlsym to get fopen, opens a file via that dlsym-resolved fopen, and passes the FILE* to FrankenLibCs fread. Verify the adoption happens (assert via the evidence ring buffer).

## Done

- adopt_foreign_file implemented
- Every stdio entrypoint and trampoline runs the bloom precheck first
- Adoption test passes
- Evidence records appear in the ring buffer for the test
- Stale-pointer EBADF path covered by a separate unit test","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:30:24.854354196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-15T21:01:08.004085692Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T21:01:08.000634834Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foreign-adoption","membrane","stdio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.16","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:30:24.854354196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.17","title":"stdio: per-FILE parking_lot::ReentrantMutex + flockfile/funlockfile/ftrylockfile + *_unlocked","description":"## Background

Plan v4 §1.11. POSIX requires every standard stdio function to be thread-safe via implicit per-FILE locking, with flockfile/funlockfile/ftrylockfile for explicit batching, and the *_unlocked family for callers that already hold the lock. v1 ignored this entirely.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1 (NativeFile must already have the lock slot)

## What to do

1. NativeFileState contains parking_lot::ReentrantMutex<NativeFileLocked>. The locked region wraps buffer cursors, eof/error flags, ungetc, mbstate, orientation, healing budget.
2. Every public ABI fn (fread, fwrite, ...) acquires the lock via reentrant_mutex.lock() before entering the kernel; releases on exit (RAII via guard).
3. Every *_unlocked variant (fread_unlocked, fwrite_unlocked, fgetc_unlocked, fputc_unlocked, fgets_unlocked, fputs_unlocked, feof_unlocked, ferror_unlocked, clearerr_unlocked, fileno_unlocked) skips the acquisition AND under #[cfg(debug_assertions)] asserts the caller already holds the lock (parking_lots ReentrantMutex exposes is_locked / try_lock for this).
4. flockfile / funlockfile / ftrylockfile (verify they exist in stdio_abi.rs; if not, add them) operate on the same recursive mutex.
5. The recursive lock count must survive setjmp/longjmp correctly (deferred to bd-9chy.18, the longjmp lock-stack bead).
6. Add an integration test that fans out 32 threads, each writing 1024 4-byte records to a shared FILE*; reads back; verifies record-level interleaving (no torn writes). Both strict and hardened modes.

## Done

- Every public ABI fn takes the lock; every *_unlocked fn does not
- ReentrantMutex used (not Mutex — POSIX requires recursive)
- 32-thread stress test passes in both modes
- flockfile/funlockfile/ftrylockfile work correctly under nested lock","notes":"Implemented flockfile/funlockfile/ftrylockfile. NativeFile already has ReentrantMutex in NativeFileState. Remaining: verify all ABI fns use locking, audit *_unlocked variants, add stress test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:30:53.015024994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-15T20:20:27.696131389Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T20:20:27.693099311Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["locking","stdio"]}

-{"id":"bd-o4yda","title":"fuzz: open/openat argument validation (path + flags + mode)","description":"## Scope
Add crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_open_syscalls.rs targeting frankenlibc_abi::fcntl_abi::{open,openat,creat} ABI wrappers — the NARROWEST input boundary where the libc replacement validates pathname pointers, O_* flag combinations, and mode_t.

## Archetype (per /testing-fuzzing)
1 + 3 combined: Crash-detector on our impl, plus **differential** against libc::open/openat for inputs that are safe to call on a disposable tmp path.

## Inputs
struct OpenFuzzInput {
  path: Vec<u8>,          // mutated from seeds: /tmp/<ok>, /dev/null, /nonexistent, ..\X, empty, embedded NUL (rejected by CString)
  flags: i32,             // O_CREAT|O_RDWR|... ANDed against the known-valid mask
  mode: u32,              // 0..=0o7777
  use_openat: bool,
  at_fd: i32,             // AT_FDCWD or a cached tmpdir fd
}

## Invariants
- Never panic on any input
- rc is either a non-negative fd OR -1 with errno in the documented set (EACCES/ENOENT/EEXIST/EISDIR/ENOTDIR/EFAULT/EINVAL/ELOOP/ENAMETOOLONG/ENOSPC/EROFS/EMFILE/ENFILE/EPERM/ENOMEM)
- For any successful open, our fd close() returns 0
- Host parity: our rc/errno family matches libc::openat when the path refers to an inert location (/tmp/<random> or /dev/null)

## Expected bug classes
- Missing flag-bit validation, O_TMPFILE misparse, O_PATH ABI gap, pathname length truncation, errno leak between calls, FD leak under error branches, membrane decision divergence for boundary addresses.

## Cleanup contract
Every successful fd must be closed before iteration ends to avoid fd exhaustion; use a per-iteration tmpdir with restoreable unlinking.

## Score (fault × independence × cost)
5 × 5 × /2 ≈ 12.5 — highest-priority fuzz target after pattern_match.

Per skill: register in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml, verify cargo +nightly fuzz run links (bd-0ubkr now closed), enforce ≥1000 exec/s (may need tmpfs for fd churn).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:39:20.475456710Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:13:08.446218796Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-o4yda","depends_on_id":"bd-jy4qu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:01:42.908929016Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-oai","title":"Epic: Dual-Mode Semantics & Deterministic Replay","description":"Dual-mode execution: Strict (default, ABI-compatible, no repair, <20ns) and Hardened (deterministic repair per policy tables, structured JSON evidence per repair, <200ns). Mode selected immutably at process startup for deterministic replay. RuntimeKernelSnapshot (154-field state capture). Evidence symbol records with RaptorQ-inspired redundancy.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Session 30: Epic closed — all children completed

All child beads closed:
- bd-oai.1: Dual-mode selection + strict/hardened execution paths ✅
- bd-oai.2: RuntimeKernelSnapshot (165+ fields) + deterministic replay ✅  
- bd-oai.3: Evidence symbol records + RaptorQ-inspired redundancy ✅

Verification:
- 16 E2E tests in runtime_math_dual_mode_e2e_test.rs (all pass)
- 33 evidence system tests (all pass)
- Deterministic replay produces identical decision streams and JSONL logs
- Branch-diversity enforcement: Healthy/NearViolation/Violation states verified
- Golden snapshot regression gate: JSON equality under deterministic inputs","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:58:48.791246202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T05:13:40.030481360Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T05:13:32Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","dual-mode","epic","frankenlibc","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-oai","depends_on_id":"bd-32e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1124,"issue_id":"bd-oai","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:03Z"}]}

+{"id":"bd-8sgm6","title":"[MODE-3][MEDIUM] ABI compatibility: printf executor hardcodes LP64 widths","description":"[MODE-3][MEDIUM] ABI compatibility: printf conformance executor hardcodes LP64 vararg widths

Recent printf executor work widened promotion and dispatch handling, but the ABI model is still host-width-specific:
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:2492-2496` stores long-like integers as `PrintfArg::Long(c_longlong)` and `PrintfArg::Ulong(c_ulonglong)`.
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:2502-2585` treats `%l`, `%j`, `%z`, `%t`, and `%q` as a single `long_like` bucket and rewrites integer args into those 64-bit slots.

That matches the current LP64 host, but it is not an ABI-stable model for width-sensitive formats such as `%ld`, `%zd`, and `%td`. On non-LP64 targets the executor can mis-marshal arguments and report false conformance parity.

This is an ABI review finding rather than a current Linux x86_64 failure, but it directly affects whether the conformance harness can be trusted when the matrix expands past today's host ABI.

Fix direction:
- split `long`, `long long`, `size_t`, `ptrdiff_t`, and `intmax_t` by actual target width
- source widths from explicit target metadata instead of one 64-bit promotion bucket.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:46:05.341784917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:07:58.289185820+00:00","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","modes","printf","review"],"closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:07:58.286927440+00:00","notes":"Closed by cc-libc as duplicate of bd-1tvbi (already closed at commit b624a988 on 2026-04-22T09:32Z).

Identical description: same file (crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs), same line ranges (2492-2585), same set of LP64-width concerns (%l, %j, %z, %t, %q bucketing into a single c_longlong slot), same fix direction (split-by-ABI-width refactor gated on target metadata).

The accepted fix in bd-1tvbi was pragmatic: make the LP64 assumption explicit via three compile-time size_of asserts (see crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:2520-2532) that fail the build on any target where c_long / size_t / ptrdiff_t is narrower than c_longlong. The three assertion messages all point back to 'refactor bd-1tvbi before building for non-LP64 targets', which bound the scope correctly: the executor stays correct on every LP64 target we currently support, and the moment a non-LP64 target is added to the matrix the build fails loudly with a direct pointer at the bead.

Re-opening bd-8sgm6 as its own refactor scope would duplicate that work. When the ABI matrix actually expands, reopen bd-1tvbi (not this bead) to do the full split-by-width refactor."}
+{"id":"bd-9chy.10","title":"stdio: export _IO_2_1_stdin_/stdout_/stderr_ aliases bound to NativeFile storage","description":"## Background

Plan v4 §3 (final paragraph). Some statically-linked-against-libc binaries resolve _IO_2_1_stdin_, _IO_2_1_stdout_, _IO_2_1_stderr_ directly via dlsym or link-time resolution, bypassing the public stdin/stdout/stderr aliases. Without exporting these names from FrankenLibC, the binary sees two distinct streams (one native FrankenLibC, one host glibc) and the program misbehaves subtly: partial flushes, lost output, double-close.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1 (NativeFile layout)

## What to do

1. Add the three symbol names to crates/frankenlibc-abi/version_scripts/libc.map under the GLIBC_2.2.5 version tag.
2. In stdio_abi.rs (or io_internal_abi.rs), declare the underscored names as #[no_mangle] static aliases pointing to the *same* NativeFile storage as the public stdin/stdout/stderr globals. Do NOT allocate a second set of NativeFile objects — they must be the same object so a flush via stdin reaches the same buffer as a flush via _IO_2_1_stdin_.
3. Verify the alias by building the .so and inspecting nm: nm -D target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so | grep _IO_2_1 must show the three symbols at the same address as stdin/stdout/stderr.
4. Add an integration test: build a small C program that takes both stdin and _IO_2_1_stdin_, asserts they are equal, and runs it under LD_PRELOAD.

## Done

- libc.map exports the three aliases
- nm verification passes
- Integration test passes
- The aliases survive a release build with strip+lto","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:28:41.061649453Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:44:25.584876183+00:00","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:44:25.582645349+00:00","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","stdio","version-script"]}
